summary: Microsoft documents NoActiveProbe = 1 as the policy state that turns off NCSI active tests. The app now writes only that official machine-policy value.

setting.name: NCSI Active Probing Policy
setting.casual_explanation: Windows checks internet connectivity by making active test requests. Turning those tests off can change how Windows decides whether a network has internet access.
setting.why_people_change_it: Admins turn this off when they want to stop Windows from making those connectivity test requests or when they are hardening or troubleshooting network detection behavior.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning the policy on stops NCSI active tests, which can make some Windows features less accurate about whether the device has internet access.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkConnectivityStatusIndicator\NoActiveProbe with enabled value 1 and disabled value 0. The app previously added extra PolicyManager and NlaSvc runtime values, but those unsourced writes were removed on 2026-03-13 so the implementation now matches the documented policy surface exactly.

allowed_values.label: Default behavior
allowed_values.meaning: Windows can continue using the normal NCSI active tests.

allowed_values.label: Policy disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled, so the normal active-test behavior remains available.

allowed_values.label: Disable active probing
allowed_values.meaning: NCSI does not run the active tests Microsoft describes.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now writes only the documented NCSI policy value HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkConnectivityStatusIndicator\NoActiveProbe = 1 for this tweak.

windows_defaults.label: NCSI default active testing
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows Vista and later policy surface documented by Microsoft

states.rationale: Microsoft says active tests run normally when the policy is not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Machines that rely on normal Windows connectivity detection
avoid_for: Admins who intentionally want to stop NCSI active tests

states.rationale: Do not force the NCSI policy unless you specifically want to stop active tests.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to continue its normal internet connectivity checks.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You have a deliberate policy reason to stop those tests.
tradeoffs: Connectivity detection remains more accurate for many Windows features.
Windows may continue making active test requests.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable active probing
intended_for: Administrators and advanced users with a deliberate network hardening or troubleshooting reason
avoid_for: Systems that rely on normal Windows internet detection behavior

states.rationale: Use the documented NoActiveProbe policy value that turns off NCSI active tests.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally want to stop Windows from performing NCSI active probes.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows features to keep ordinary internet detection behavior.
tradeoffs: NCSI active tests are turned off.
Some Windows features may become less accurate about internet access.

evidence.title: Microsoft administrative template for NCSI active probing
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents NoActiveProbe under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkConnectivityStatusIndicator with enabled value 1 and disabled value 0.

evidence.title: Microsoft help text for NCSI active probing policy
evidence.summary: Microsoft explains that enabling the policy turns off active NCSI tests and warns that other components may become less able to determine internet access.

evidence.title: Current network tweak provider
evidence.summary: The current app writes NoActiveProbe = 1 for this tweak.

evidence.title: nohuto mirror - NCSI NoActiveProbe registry evidence
evidence.summary: The local nohuto mirror records PolicyName and ValueName as NoActiveProbe and includes the registry path \Registry\Machine\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\WINDOWS\NetworkConnectivityStatusIndicator : NoActiveProbe.

decision.why: The NoActiveProbe policy is backed by exact Microsoft ADMX and ADML proof, and the app now writes only that documented machine-policy surface. The tweak is reversible and publishable, but it is not recommended for general users because it can make Windows connectivity detection less accurate.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="NoActiveProbe" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkConnectivityStatusIndicator" valueName="NoActiveProbe">; ICM.adml: This policy setting turns off the active tests performed by the Windows Network Connectivity Status Indicator (NCSI) to determine whether your computer is connected to the Internet or to a more limited network.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX and ADML files document the exact NoActiveProbe policy surface. The app now matches that documented machine-policy surface exactly. The local nohuto mirror independently corroborates the same policy name, value name, and registry path.
